response:
In C# it is possible to estimate the time it takes for a UDP packet to reach a server  
using the Stopwatch class  
you can send a packet and then wait for a response  
however UDP does not guarantee delivery or response  
so you cannot always measure the round trip time  
you can only measure the time between sending and receiving a response  
if a response is received  
you can calculate the time difference  
this is similar to ping but only for one direction  
you need to handle the socket properly  
and ensure the server is configured to respond  
the code involves creating a UDP client  
sending a message  
and listening for a reply  
you can use the SendTo and ReceiveFrom methods  
to send and receive data  
you can also use the Timestamp option in the socket  
to get more accurate timing  
but this may not be supported on all systems  
you should also handle exceptions and timeouts  
to avoid blocking the application  
in summary it is possible but requires careful handling of the socket and response  
you can measure the time between sending and receiving a reply  
if a reply is received
